because.text: First external acceptance-gate audit of the architect-mode hardening (Gemini 3.1 Pro High via agy). Most findings were warrant-side (2 verifier crashes + a fuzzer blind spot, adjudicated there). Sigma-side: (1) the two Ed25519 tools (warrant_verify.py, anchor_governance.py) add the 2 non-canonical sign-bit torsion encodings the auditor named -- current libs already reject them at decode (verified 0/128 in Python AND Go), so belt-and-suspenders for a lenient third impl. (2) book1_fuzz previously capped every atp at EVAL_CAP, blinding the three-engine differential to the uint32 boundary; it now emits a genuine 2^32-1 budget for terms that halt within EVAL_CAP, so Python/Rust/warrant-go are all exercised at the integer boundary. Refuted findings (ATP force wrap, scalar-record crash) verified false. cmd@v1 re-runs all Book suites, both differentials, governance selftest, warrant_verify, anchors, Rust + warrant-go conformance, and the three-engine fuzzer (2 seeds); transcript pinned. Sibling adjudication filed in warrant as c321e5a7.

subject.note: Gemini 3.1 Pro external audit (cross-repo): sigma tools get 2 non-canonical torsion keys as defense-in-depth; book1_fuzz now sends 2^32-1 budgets to all 3 Book I engines. Green.
